playbooks.name: Remote Access / Tech Support Scam
playbooks.description: Adaptive questioning for remote access, tech support impersonation, and account compromise scenarios.

factors.name: Remote software installed
factors.question: Did anyone ask you to install remote access or screen-sharing software on your phone or computer?
factors.followUpYes: What software did they ask you to install, such as AnyDesk, TeamViewer, LogMeIn, Zoho Assist, or something similar?
factors.resolutionHint: Advise the customer to disconnect the session and uninstall the remote software.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Active remote session still running
factors.question: Is anyone currently connected to your device or still able to see your screen?
factors.followUpYes: Please disconnect from Wi-Fi or mobile data if needed and close the remote access app before proceeding.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Logged into bank during remote session
factors.question: Did you log into your bank, email, or crypto accounts while the other person could see your screen?
factors.followUpYes: We should treat your credentials as potentially compromised.
factors.resolutionHint: Force credential reset and escalate for fraud review.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Instructed to move money
factors.question: Did they tell you to move money, buy gift cards, send a wire, or transfer funds to protect your account?
factors.followUpYes: That is a major scam indicator.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Coached, rushed, or threatened
factors.question: Did they tell you not to speak with your bank, or pressure you to act quickly?
factors.followUpYes: That behavior is consistent with coercion.
factors.resolutionHint: Escalate and use a warning script.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

hardStops: customer says a remote access app is still connected
customer moved money to protect an account
customer says they were told not to speak with the bank
customer logged into bank or crypto while screen sharing

playbooks.name: Bank Wire Request Review
playbooks.description: Adaptive questioning for consumer and small business wire requests.

factors.name: New beneficiary
factors.question: Is this the first time you are sending money to this recipient?
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Payment instructions changed
factors.question: Did the payment instructions or account number change recently, especially by email or text?
factors.followUpYes: Changed instructions can indicate business email compromise or impersonation.
factors.resolutionHint: Hold and verify with a trusted number.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Urgency or secrecy language
factors.question: Has anyone told you this payment must be sent urgently or kept confidential?
factors.followUpYes: Urgency and secrecy are elevated fraud indicators.
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: Coached by third party
factors.question: Are you currently on the phone or messaging with someone who is guiding this transfer?
factors.followUpYes: Live coaching is a significant warning sign.
positiveCues: someone is walking me through it
factors.followUpUnsure: If you are not fully sure, treat this as unresolved risk and gather a little more detail before moving on.

factors.name: No independent verification
factors.question: Have you independently verified the recipient using a trusted number or known contact, not from the message requesting payment?
factors.resolutionHint: If no, challenge or hold the wire pending verification.
factors.followUpUnsure: Please pause and verify the recipient using a trusted number or previously known contact before proceeding.

hardStops: wire instructions changed by email or text only
client is being coached live by another party
client says the transfer must be secret or urgent
client cannot verify the recipient independently
